summary: Right direction, but the retry behavior isn't production-safe yet. Also: there are no tests — cover the backoff schedule and the give-up path before this lands.

comments.body: Did you consider tenacity instead of hand-rolling this?

discussion.body: Did you consider tenacity instead of hand-rolling this?
I did — but the project keeps runtime dependencies at zero (stdlib + requests only), so I hand-rolled the ~15 lines. tenacity would be the right call otherwise.

comments.reply: Kept stdlib-only per the discussion — tenacity stays out.

comments.body: A fixed 1-second sleep will hammer an API that's already struggling. Use exponential backoff with jitter.
comments.reply: Fixed — exponential backoff (base 0.5 s, cap 8 s) with full jitter.

comments.body: Retrying on bare Exception hides real bugs — a TypeError or a 404 gets retried ten times. Catch only connection errors and 5xx responses.
comments.reply: Now retries only requests.ConnectionError and 5xx HTTPError; everything else raises immediately.

comments.body: When every attempt fails we surface the last error with no sign that retries happened — consider a WARNING log per retry.
comments.reply: Not changed: kept the helper log-free — callers own observability; the docstring now says so.
